Request Private Consultation

Interior & Wall Systems

Interior renovations that keep one finish language from room to room.

For selective whole-home upgrades, the studio combines tiling, carpentry and internal wall-system work into one coherent brief.

Manchester North West - Lancashire Plan / Design / Craft

Service Overview

A joined-up scope for interiors, carpentry and wall systems.

This service extends the studio beyond bathrooms and kitchens into broader interior work where carpentry, tiling and internal wall systems need to read as one package.

  • Joinery, trim and carpentry detail tied into the main design direction.
  • Internal wall systems planned for straight lines and dependable performance.
  • Exterior wall upgrades discussed where envelope and appearance overlap.
  • Selective multi-room sequencing managed under one premium brief.

Service Priorities

How the studio keeps this scope premium.

01

Room-to-room continuity

Finishes and transitions are resolved as one composition rather than isolated upgrades.

02

Technical discipline

Wall build-ups, trims and service routes are considered before closing works begin.

03

Finish restraint

The goal is composed, durable spaces rather than decorative noise.

Case Study

North West interior refresh

A multi-room brief combining carpentry, internal wall work and surface refinements to create one calmer architectural language throughout the property.

Selective multi-room sequencing Joinery and wall build-ups Coordinated material palette

Material Language

Gallery references for this service line.

FAQ

Common questions before the brief is shaped.

Do you take on work beyond bathrooms and kitchens?

Yes. We handle selected interior scopes where carpentry, tiling and wall systems are central to the outcome.

Are internal and external wall systems quoted separately?

They can be scoped separately or folded into a broader interior brief depending on the project.

Who is this service for?

Clients who want a consistent premium language across more than one room or across the building fabric itself.

Direct Contact

Discuss interior & wall systems directly with the studio.

The same direct phones and studio email from the business card are surfaced here so broader interior enquiries can move without friction.
